cap-a-pie.def: 從頭到腳、全副武裝的人的人物、完整地 (全)
cap-a-pie.ex: The knight was armed cap-a-pie for the grand tournament.

caparison.def: 馬飾、(女)裝飾、遮蓋、裝飾的人的人、裝飾地 (飾)
caparison.ex: The royal horse was draped in a rich gold caparison.

cape.def: 披肩、斗篷、岬、海角的人物 (單數)
cape.col: red riding cape, cape of good hope
cape.ex: She wore a heavy red riding cape to keep out the cold.

caper.def: 跳躍、惡作劇、(俚)案件、蹦跳、惡作劇的人的人、蹦跳地 (跳)
caper.ex: The young goats were cutting capers in the lush meadow.

capillarity.ex: Capillarity helps plants to draw water from the soil.

capillary.def: 毛細管的、毛髮狀的、細管的人物 (單數)
capillary.ex: Blood flows from arteries into the small capillary vessels.

capitalism.ex: Modern capitalism has drive massive global economic growth.

capitalist.def: 資本主義的、資本家的、資本家的人的人物、資本家地 (資)
capitalist.ex: The venture capitalist decided to invest in the tech startup.

capitalistic.def: 資本主義的、資本家的的人物
capitalistic.ex: Our society is built on a complex capitalistic system.

capitalization.def: 資本化、大寫、資本額 (單數)
capitalization.ex: The company's market capitalization reached a record high.

capitalize.def: 資本化、大寫、利用、獲利、獲利的人的人、獲利地 (利)
capitalize.ex: We need to capitalize on this unique business opportunity.

capitally.def: 首要地、出色地、處以死刑地
capitally.ex: The young actor behaved capitally throughout the long rehearsal.

capitation.ex: The hospital receives a capitation grant for each local patient.

capitol.def: (美)州議會大廈、美國國會大廈人物 (單數)
capitol.ex: The protesters gathered on the steps of the state capitol tonight.

capitulate.def: (有條件地)投降、屈服的人的人、屈服地 (降)
capitulate.ex: The besieged army finally decided to capitulate at dawn.

capitulation.ex: There was news of the unconditional capitulation of the rebels.

capon.ex: They served a delicious roast capon for the holiday feast.

capote.ex: The traveler wrapped his heavy capote tightly around him.

capper.def: 蓋帽者、壓頂石、裝蓋的小工具、結尾的人、蓋頂地 (完)
capper.ex: The final goal was a real capper to a great season.

cappuccino.ex: She ordered a frothy cappuccino with a sprinkle of cocoa.

capping.def: 蓋、封頂、封閉的人 (或物) (封)
capping.ex: The engineers are working on the capping of the oil well.

caprice.def: 反覆無常、任性、怪想的人物 (單數)
caprice.ex: Her decision to leave seemed like a sudden, childish caprice.

capricious.def: 反覆無常的、任性的的人物
capricious.ex: The capricious mountain weather can change in a few minutes.

capriciously.ex: He acted capriciously, changing his mind every half hour.

capsize.def: (船)翻覆、弄翻、翻覆的人的人、翻地 (翻)
capsize.ex: The small boat began to capsize in the rough harbor waves.

capstan.ex: The sailors used the heavy capstan to raise the anchor.

capsule.def: 膠囊、太空艙、小容器、概括的人的人物、簡短地 (膠囊)
capsule.col: vitamin capsule, space capsule, in capsule form
capsule.ex: He swallowed the vitamin capsule with a gulp of water.

captain.def: 隊長、船長、上尉、首領、指揮的人的人物、領導地 (隊長)
captain.col: team captain, sea captain, captaincy skills
captain.ex: The team captain led his players onto the field.

captaincy.def: 隊長職位、首領地位 (單數)
captaincy.ex: He was proud to assume the captaincy of the national team.

caption.def: 標題、字幕、說明、加上標題的人的人物、加上標地 (標)
caption.ex: The photo caption explained that it was taken in 1920.

captivate.def: 迷住、迷惑、擄獲的人的人、迷惑地 (迷)
captivate.ex: Her beautiful singing continued to captivate the audience.

captivating.def: 迷人的、有魅力的的人 (或物) (迷)
captivating.ex: She has a truly captivating smile that lights up the room.

captivation.ex: The children watched the magician in utter captivation.

captivator.ex: He is a natural captivator who wins friends easily.
